message:
fix(pthread): resolve self-join deadlock by spin-waiting for TID publication

Fix a race condition in join_thread() where self-join detection could
miss the EDEADLK check during the clone startup window before the
parent-side TID publication is visible in the handle's tid field.

The problem: when a child thread calls pthread_join(self), there is a
small window after clone() where handle.tid is still 0 because the
parent hasn't published it yet. The existing self-join check compared
my_tid against target_tid=0 and fell through, causing the thread to
block forever waiting on itself.

The fix: when target_tid is 0 and the thread is still in STARTING or
RUNNING state, spin-wait (with core::hint::spin_loop()) until either
the TID is published or the thread transitions to a terminal state.
This ensures the self-join comparison always sees a valid TID when the
thread is alive.

Also add pthread_self_join_is_rejected_with_edeadlk test that spawns a
child which calls pthread_join(self) and verifies the child receives
EDEADLK, confirming the fix end-to-end.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
